title: NODE 014 — The River Recording

features.name: the microphone stand, still standing
features.desc-on-touch: Someone recorded this river at 1500 hours in the late twentieth century and never came back for the stand. The river kept talking to it.

features.name: the river, at 1500 hours
features.desc-on-touch: You listen. Under the water there is a slow rotary turning, blade after blade, like a fan in a motel room in 1987. The file insists it has always sounded like this.

features.name: a casing lashed to a river oak
features.desc-on-touch: The LED blinks in three-quarter time. checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable.

features.name: a spectrogram, printed and nailed to a post
features.desc-on-touch: Spectrogram_RiverAnomaly.png. Someone has circled a band of frequencies in biro and written: THIS IS NOT THE RIVER.

inscription: a field near one town returns as a riverbank near another. the archive files them together now

file.name: River_NSW_1500hrs_Late20thCent.mp3
file.note: 38 minutes of river. At 22:10 a ceiling fan enters, and someone breathing. status: germinating

exits.label: the fire trail back to the daisy field
upstream, where the water sounds rehearsed
a line of biro arrows on fence posts
exits.to: NEW: the paddock of fence-post annotations, every post carrying one word of a sentence nobody has walked in full
